result:
Done. All three operations are implemented in `CsvRow.calr` (the Calor DSL source), the project builds cleanly, and behavior is verified against the compiled assembly — all 16 checks pass, including a 2000-case randomized `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields)) == fields` round-trip property.

What I added, following the conventions of the existing `NeedsQuoting` function (`§F` module functions, explicit IDs, pure `§E{}` effects):

- **`EscapeField`** (`§E{}`, pure) — returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes with `replace` and wraps the result in quotes.
- **`JoinRow`** (`§E{}`, pure) — walks the list with a while loop, escaping each field via `EscapeField` and inserting commas between fields; the empty list yields the empty string.
- **`SplitRow`** (declared `§E{mut}` because building the result list with `§PUSH` requires the mutation effect under the project's effects-enforcement pin; it's still pure computation with no I/O or global state) — parses one field per outer-loop iteration: a field starting with `"` is scanned to its closing un-doubled quote (doubled quotes contribute one literal quote; commas/CR/LF inside are content), otherwise the field ends at the next comma or end of line. A trailing comma produces a trailing empty field, and the empty line parses to a single empty field.

Two build cycles were used out of the budget of 10: the first failed because an empty `§LIST{fields:str}` block doesn't parse without items, so I switched to `§B{fields:List<str>} §NEW{List<str>} §/NEW`; the second built cleanly and I confirmed the generated C# matches the intended algorithm before running the behavioral checks.
